title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 261 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Oil prices held steady at $72 the tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased.

title: Indian American Billionaire CEO Says Half Of These Jobs Will Disappear
content: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora says AI could halve corporate teams in marketing, HR and finance over the next three years, while demand for AI-savvy workers grows.

title: Aadhaar App Launches Email Update Feature, Service To Be Free For 6 Months
content: "This is a free service via the Aadhaar App only, effective 1 July 2026 for a period of six months, making it easier than ever for residents to keep their Aadhaar information up to date," the release said.

title: Opinion: Opinion | How Texas Became Hub Of A Terror-Link Kashmiri Outfit's Activities
content: Across the state, a collection of terror networks and their Islamist partners are using US politicians, institutions and nonprofit infrastructure to advance violent, radical ambitions.

title: Groww, Zerodha, Upstox Get GIFT City Approval. But A Bigger Challenge Awaits
content: According to Kinjal Shah, CA and Vice President of the Bombay Chartered Accountants Society, that is now the defining challenge for GIFT City.

title: 40 Dead As Bus Plunges Into Ravine In Pakistan's Balochistan
content: A bus carrying stranded passengers from a broken-down vehicle went out of control in Balochistan, marking one of Pakistan's deadliest road crashes.

title: France Records 30% Rise In Deaths During June Heatwave: Report
content: Deaths in France jumped nearly 30% during June's record heatwave, with Paris hospitals bracing for a toll that could rival the deadly 2003 crisis

title: 'America250': All About July 4 US Independence Anniversary Celebrations
content: Ahead of the grand day on July 4, activities have already begun across the nation, including commemorative events and nationwide campaigns.

title: Drought Conditions Spread Across India, Farmers Put On Edge
content: A comparison of the last week of June 2025 and the last week of June 2026 shows a clear increase in drought-affected regions.

title: US Declared Its Independence On July 2, But Why Is It Celebrated On July 4?
content: Although the Continental Congress voted for independence on July 2, 1776, July 4 became America's official Independence Day. Here's why the two dates continue to spark historical debate.

title: US Teen Lost Her Hand, Leg In Shark Attack. Now An Alert System Is Named After Her
content: Inspired by Alabama teen Lulu Gribbin who lost two limbs in a 2024 Florida shark bite, a new US law greenlights immediate smartphone emergency alerts.

title: Ketan Agarwal Murder Probe Widens, Chetan Chaudhary's Classmate Under Scanner
content: The families of Siya and Ketan had fixed their marriage, and the wedding was scheduled for November this year.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Pak Needs To Understand: Its Indus Treaty 'Threats' To India Are Fooling No One
content: Pakistan's hysterics and fits haven't impressed India, much less make her re-consider her stand on the treaty. If Islamabad is trying to threaten its way to water, it is already failing.

title: Why Was Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral Delayed And Where Was His Body Till Now?
content: There have been rumours that his body was temporarily buried, but Iranian officials have insisted the delay was due to severe, volatile conditions of the active war with the United States and Israel.

title: Hyderabad Woman Checks Into Hotel, Dies By Suicide On Video Call With Friend
content: According to investigators, Renuka had checked into the hotel in Hyderabad's Langar Houz area with a man at around 3 am on Wednesday.

title: Bandra is disappearing: Is this what Mumbai calls development?
content: The suburb is changing fast. Will redevelopment transform Mumbai into a city of identical towers instead of distinct neighbourhoods?

title: GoDaddy fears government’s fake website crackdown could make the internet less safe
content: The legal dispute embroiling GoDaddy and others was triggered ‌by more than 20 companies that sought the court’s intervention on fake websites damaging their brand

title: ‘Gatta Kusthi 2’ movie review: Double the fun, same old pitfalls
content: With sharper writing, Chella Ayyavu’s sequel film improves over the original, only to repeat its biggest mistakes when it matters most

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Thunderstorms, lightning likely across Jharkhand, IMD issues orange alert
content: The IMD issued an ‘orange’ alert for isolated thunderstorms and lightning across all 24 districts in Jharkhand on July 4, followed by a ‘yellow’ alert from July 6 to 8

title: Mar Thoma Church head opposes Kerala govt’s move to cut tax on low-alcohol beverages
content: Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church head Theodosius Mar Thoma Metropolitan says the Church is against all forms of liquor

title: Plea in Supreme Court seeks an end to ‘coaching Raj’, dummy school nexus
content: Plea urges court to direct authorities to “dismantle the parallel, unregulated, fee-driven private coaching ecosystem” and align the curriculum and testing patterns of national entrance examinations with state-prescribed school syllabus

title: Ram temple donation theft: RSS calls incident ‘unfortunate’, urges patience and strict action
content: It is essential to ensure that whoever is found guilty in the investigation receives strict punishment, says RSS General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale

title: Chessworld AI becomes official technology partner of Karnataka State Chess Association, start-up supported by Elevate
content: Chessworld AI will introduce an integrated digital platform for tournament registrations, player and tournament management, digitisation of over-the-board games, AI-powered game analysis, and personalised learning tools for players.

title: Kochi Corporation’s health standing committee to consider two proposals to use drones for mosquito control
content: A start-up has submitted a proposal for employing drones for spraying larvicides. Another company proposes operating drones to spray an organic compound at mosquito-breeding grounds identified through GIS mapping

title: Wife's 'thousand husbands' barb 'grave provocation' that led to her killing: HC cuts man's life term
content: "When a wife refers to her husband that 'she can keep thousand husbands like him' it is indirect/oblique reference to worthlessness of the husband, meaning he has no value as a human being or a husband; therefore, it can be turned as a sudden and grave provocation"

title: DMK moves Supreme Court to bar TVK leaders from making ‘threatening’ public statements on Karur stampede
content: The DMK petition has pointed out that Chief Minister Vijay’s proposed visit to Karur has the potential to give rise to an apprehension, regarding the fairness and independence of the investigative process.

title: ‘When all else fails, people still repose their trust in the judiciary’: INDIA bloc leaders in letter to CJI
content: Signed by leaders of 24 Opposition parties, including independent MP Kapil Sibal, it detailed concerns over the SIR in West Bengal and Bihar; the leaders also made a direct accusation, alleging that they believed recently conducted elections in Delhi, Haryana, and Maharashtra had been “manipulated”

title: Arvind Kejriwal urges PM to rollback of E20 fuel programme
content: Referring to concerns over fuel efficiency, he claimed that many people were reporting a decline in mileage after switching to E20 petrol

title: Shashi Tharoor recalls past security concerns amid Adani’s proposed MSC stake transfer in Vizhinjam port
content: Congress MP says Vizhinjam project had failed to secure bidders several times under both UPA and NDA governments due to concerns over possible involvement of Chinese companies

title: Watch: HPV vaccination roll out, climate change, metabolic diseases and more | Health Wrap
content: In this episode of Health Wrap, senior health journalists Ramya Kannan and Zubeda Hamid discuss the week’s important developments in public health. This episode looks at the rollout of Human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination across States in India to prevent cervical cancer, which continues to claim thousands of lives each year.

title: Can the ED attach a company’s assets after it enters insolvency? | Explained
content: The legal question before the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal was whether the protection available under the IBC moratorium extends to assets that are simultaneously the subject of proceedings under the PMLA

title: Digital arrest scam: CBI arrests three accused after searches in Odisha and Rajasthan
content: The accused have been identified as Bibekananda Dixit and Jayant Kumar Acharya from Balasore, Odisha; and Kanhaiya Lal from Nagaur, Rajasthan

title: Watch: Falling fertility rate, antibiotic resistance and a rare brain infection in Kerala
content: We unpack India’s falling fertility rate and its far-reaching social and economic consequences, examine the growing threat of antibiotic resistance, and assess the impact of recent GST reforms on healthcare. Our Kerala correspondent reports on the challenges of covering cases of amoebic meningoencephalitis, and psychiatrist Dr. Lakshmi Vijayakumar highlights the urgent need for open conversations on youth and mental health.

title: ₹35-crore TVK MLA bribery case: Senthilbalaji’s brother Ashok Kumar moves Madras High Court for anticipatory bail
content: Claims that he has no connection whatsoever with the case and that he had been roped into the matter with a political motive

title: Watch: From Uranium in Breast Milk to Superbugs: AMR Week Explained | Health Wrap
content: In this episode, we will discuss India’s new national plan to combat antimicrobial resistance. On India’s new national plan to combat antimicrobial resistance, uranium being found in the breastmilk of mothers in Bihar, the efficacy of the anti-rabies vaccine, and more.

title: Govt lens on e-rickshaw remote shutdown issue; two apps removed from app stores
content: The issue drew public attention after reports surfaced that some e-rickshaws were being rendered inoperable through a remote shutdown feature linked to the Chinese smartphone application BAT-BMS

title: Madras High Court denies advance bail to Anitha R. Radhakrishnan for his objectionable remarks against CM Vijay
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan asks how can a former Minister and a sitting MLA use such language against an elected Chief Minister

title: Bengaluru creche abuse case: Caregiver arrested, more arrests likely
content: The case came to light after purported videos showing the alleged mistreatment of children at the creche, Little Buds Day Care, surfaced on social media

title: ASI finds Erode’s Kalingarayan Canal aqueduct structurally stable
content: It concluded that the only significant issue affecting the monument is water seepage through masonry joints, which can be arrested through conservation measures

title: Jana Sena extends deadline to July 5 for party posts in Vizianagaram
content: The party has received about 1,200 applications in five days, many from doctors, lawyers and engineers, and expects the number to cross 1,500 by Sunday

title: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan arrested over objectionable remarks against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: The arrest came shortly after the Madras High Court dismissed an anticipatory bail plea filed by Mr. Radhakrishnan in a case registered by the Authoor police

title: Supreme Court refuses to stay bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi in honeymoon murder case
content: While indicating that it had “reservations” about the reasoning adopted by the High Court, the top court said it was equally conscious of the implications of sending an accused back to custody after release on bail

title: Kerala’s Hindu Aikya Vedi president booked for alleged anti-Muslim comments over Operation Toofan
content: Case registered based on complaint by PDP leader Ashraf Vazhakkala following Babu’s alleged remarks in a social media post

title: Why Bengaluru is India’s deadliest metropolitan city for deaths arising from civic negligence
content: For six consecutive years, Bengaluru has recorded the highest number of deaths due to civic negligence among metropolitan cities. Has much changed on the ground with major administrative overhauls? Chetan B C explores.

title: Four of a wedding party, including a child, killed in lorry crash near Kambham in Markapuram district
content: The four from Giddaluru had come to attend a wedding and had stopped at a roadside temple when the lorry struck their auto in the early hours of Friday; seven others were hurt

title: Karnataka app-based workers union to legally oppose attempt to strike down Gig Workers Act
content: The Union contended that the petitioners’ argument claiming that the law is repugnant to Centre’s Code on Social Security (COSS), 2020, is merely a delay tactic to evade corporate responsibility.

title: Arunachal Minister urges Centre to hand over strategic road project to BRO
content: Kento Jini He said the road was originally constructed, managed, and maintained by the BRO before being transferred to the NHIDCL during 2014-15

title: Act against tobacco syndicates to curb profiteering: Atchannaidu to Tobacco Board
content: The Minister asked the Tobacco Board to watch for price-capping on good-quality bales, and said the government would arrange credit for farmers who store their tobacco

title: Ayodhya temple donations row: Uddhav announces ‘Ram Raksha’ protest in Maharashtra from July 5
content: I appeal to all those agitated over this alleged large-scale theft at the Ram temple to join us and hold the BJP accountable for it, says Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray

title: Attack on ED officials: Kerala HC dismisses plea for CBI probe
content: Division Bench notes ‘substantial progress’ by Kerala Police in investigation into the attack and holds there is no need to direct a CBI probe at this stage

title: Enforcement of zero-liquid discharge norms in Kanpur tanneries helps curb pollution in Ganga across Uttar Pradesh
content: Kanpur’s Jajmau tannery belt is significant as it is surveyed as the largest single point source of industrial pollution on the Ganga main stem

title: Why is SpiceJet not operating from Chennai anymore? | Explained
content: The airline’s available fleet has shrunk to around 12-13 aircraft from more than 30 a few months ago, forcing the temporary suspension of services to several destinations, including Chennai, Guwahati and Varanasi.

title: Boulder collapse near Bengaluru: Half-eaten meals remain where a routine workday at quarry turned fatal
content: Most of the workers employed at the quarry are migrant labourers from Yadgir and Raichur in Karnataka, and from Jharkhand, Bihar and Madhya Pradesh

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru
content: The seven deceased include five workers from M.P., and one each from Chhattisgarh and Karnataka; owners and supervisors of adjacent quarries charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 18
content: Currently, 14 women are undergoing treatment – one each on ventilator support and oxygen support, and 12 stable

title: Fishermen warned off Andhra Pradesh coast as heavy rain, strong winds forecast
content: The Cyclone Warning Centre has forecast heavy rain in Srikakulam, Vizianagaram and Alluri Sitharama Raju districts, and winds up to 65 kmph off the coast, over the next four days

title: Operation Numkhor: Customs may summon Dulquer Salmaan again for questioning
content: Among the 60-odd vehicles seized by Customs under the drive are three vehicles owned by Dulquer Salmaan and one which has another owner but which the agency suspects is retained by the actor

title: Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 17
content: Of the 83 persons who were affected in the ammonia gas leak that occurred on June 21, 15 women are still hospitalised — one on ventilator support.

title: BJP urges Karnataka Chief Minister to declare drought in affected taluks and take relief measures on war footing
content: Leader of Opposition in the Assembly R. Ashok has stated that drought situation had gripped about 150 taluks of the State following inadequate rainfall under the southwest monsoon

title: PM Modi to make first official visit to New Zealand next week
content: The two nations signed a free ​trade ‌agreement in April that will eliminate 95% of tariffs ‌on goods from ​New Zealand

title: Jupally challenges KTR and Harish to open debate on Telangana’s debts
content: Excise Minister said Telangana’s total outstanding liabilities increased from ₹90,161 crore at the time of State formation in June 2014 to ₹8,21,651 crore by December 1, 2023, when the BRS left office

title: Do not hide data on crime like previous regime: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari tells Police
content: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari inaugurated a dedicated help desk for women as well as a cybercrime help desk across all police stations in the State

title: U.P. pushes policy reforms, cluster-based development approach to transform the poultry ecosystem
content: The poultry sector in Uttar Pradesh has witnessed significant growth over the last decade, resulting in a substantial improvement in egg availability for consumers

title: Lokesh lays foundation for ₹6.30-crore works at Mangalagiri Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy temple
content: The works include a new Yagasala and the renovation of the Vahanasala, Ghanta Mandapam and Sri Krishna Devaraya Mandapam; the Minister asked officials to draw up a master plan for the Mangalagiri temple

title: Places of Worship Act does not bar acquisition of religious sites for public purpose, says Allahabad HC
content: The observation was made by a Division Bench of Justice J.J. Munir and Justice Arun Kumar while dismissing a petition filed by six Muslim shopkeepers from Varanasi’s Dalmandi area challenging the proposed road-widening project linked to the Kashi Vishwanath Corridor

title: Cauvery realisation by Tamil Nadu in June back to the usual deficit pattern
content: The State realised a little less than three thousand million cubic feet, as against the stipulated 9.19 tmc ft for the month

title: AMMA crisis: Actor Shwetha Menon says ‘will continue’ as president till next election
content: In a social media post, she alleges that some 10 to 15 members had attended AMMA general body meeting on Thursday ‘with an agenda’ to oust her panel

title: Honeymoon murder case: Why SC refused to cancel Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail order
content: The Supreme Court declined to revoke bail for Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of her husband's murder during their honeymoon. The Meghalaya High Court had granted bail due to a significant clerical error where police cited a non-existent legal section, failing to properly inform her of the murder charge. Despite reservations, the apex court noted her release and adherence to bail conditions.

title: Govt orders removal of 3 Chinese apps being 'misused' in remotely disabling e-rickshaws
content: Authorities have removed two smartphone applications, including the Chinese BAT-BMS app, from app stores following reports of e-rickshaws being remotely disabled. These apps, designed for battery management, were allegedly exploited to disrupt vehicles by connecting to unsecured Bluetooth-enabled battery systems. The government is urging app stores to enhance scrutiny to prevent the availability of such potentially harmful applications.

title: 'Ireland hangover': Karthik on Shreyas Iyer's toss decision vs England
content: India's surprising decision to bat first in the opening T20I against England, despite overcast conditions, is attributed by Dinesh Karthik to a lingering 'Ireland hangover' from their recent series defeat. The team posted a strong 189/7 before rain abandoned the match. Captain Shreyas Iyer also achieved a personal milestone, crossing 5,000 international runs during his impressive knock.

title: How Centre aims to reduce dependence on imported gold
content: The proposal has gained traction following multiple meetings held over the past two weeks involving senior government ministers, officials from the Reserve Bank of India, banks and representatives of the gold industry, the report said.

title: One of China’s biggest ecommerce companies bans Claude Code from July 10
content: Alibaba has banned Anthropic's Claude Code from its offices starting July 10, 2026, citing 'high-risk software' and potential backdoors. Internal assessments flagged concerns after reports suggested the tool covertly checked user locations and affiliations with Chinese AI labs. This move escalates tensions, following Anthropic's accusation of Alibaba's Qwen lab using fraudulent accounts for data scraping.

title: 'Pained & angered': RSS fumes over Ram Temple row, warns of 'anti-Hindu conspiracies'
content: The RSS expressed deep pain and anger over alleged donation embezzlement at the Ayodhya Ram Temple, demanding strict action against culprits. RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale urged the Hindu community to remain vigilant against those exploiting the incident to defame them. He welcomed the SIT probe and stressed the need for transparent temple administration to uphold devotees' faith.

title: A glimpse of future combat aviation: F-15EX, Ghost Bat team up over Pacific
content: The US Air Force is pioneering Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCAs), or loyal wingmen, as part of its sixth-generation fighter program. These autonomous drones, like the MQ-28 Ghost Bat, are being tested in the Pacific alongside manned jets. India is also developing its own loyal wingman, the CATS Warrior, to enhance its fighter fleet's capabilities in future aerial combat scenarios.

title: After Dell's $6.25 bn Trump Accounts donation, chip giant Micron gifts millions
content: Micron is putting $250 million into Trump Accounts, matching employee contributions up to $1,000 per child and seeding $250 for kids in its operating communities across seven states. Trump called it the "biggest corporate investment of its kind," days before the accounts open for contributions on July 4. The pledge follows Dell's $6.25 billion donation and adds to Micron's existing $200 billion US manufacturing commitment.

title: 102 missed alerts, failed anti-drone system: US report on 2024 Trump shooting
content: A critical report reveals the US Secret Service missed crucial local police radio chatter about the gunman who targeted President Trump in Pennsylvania. The agency also lacked a joint communications hub, receiving minimal alerts. This failure, coupled with an inoperable counter-drone system that missed a pre-attack drone flight, left the President's security vulnerable. The incident resulted in casualties and injuries.

title: Thali removal not enough: Madras High Court grants divorce for mental cruelty
content: A Madras High Court ruling granted a husband a divorce, citing mental cruelty. While the wife admitted to removing her thali chain during separation, the court clarified this act alone isn't grounds for divorce. However, it served as crucial evidence, alongside public accusations and prolonged separation, indicating the couple's intent to end the marriage irrevocably. The judgment emphasizes context over symbolic acts in divorce proceedings.

title: Germany tightens workplace rules, no sick leaves over call or text
content: Germany is tightening its sick leave policy, requiring a doctor's note from day one of illness, a move Chancellor Friedrich Merz believes will boost the economy. Previously, employees enjoyed up to six weeks of paid sick leave per illness. This reform, alongside increased retirement age and flexible hiring, aims to enhance national competitiveness. However, doctors warn of potential strain on the healthcare system.

title: Why OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon & now Microsoft is sending employees to customers' offices
content: Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI and Anthropic are all racing to embed thousands of engineers directly inside customer offices, betting nearly $9 billion combined that AI only delivers real value with hands-on deployment. Microsoft's $2.5 billion Frontier Company is the latest and largest such move, following OpenAI's $4 billion Deployment Company, Anthropic's $1.5 billion venture and Amazon's $1 billion AWS unit, signalling a new phase in enterprise AI.

title: 'Dhoni sir is very similar': Deepti compares India's legend to Djokovic
content: Deepti Sharma compared MS Dhoni to Novak Djokovic, saying both legends share an exceptional ability to remain calm under pressure. Attending The Championships, Wimbledon for the first time, Deepti called it a dream come true, named Djokovic her favourite tennis player, and said she would love to watch tennis alongside Sachin Tendulkar, Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar.

title: Indian Navy sends P-8I to RIMPAC, the world's largest naval exercise
content: India's P-8I aircraft is participating in RIMPAC, the world's largest naval exercise, hosted by the US. This triennial event, themed 'Partners: Integrated and Prepared,' aims to enhance interoperability and readiness among thirty participating nations. The exercise features diverse training scenarios, underscoring the strong, ongoing cooperation between the Indian and US navies in various operational domains and humanitarian efforts.

title: 'We were fortunate': Martinez defends VAR review; Croatia coach laments 'bad refereeing'
content: Portugal coach Roberto Martinez defended the VAR decisions in his side's dramatic 2-1 World Cup win over Croatia, insisting "there were no bad decisions" and backing the use of ball technology. Croatia boss Zlatko Dalic criticised the officiating as "very bad refereeing" but admitted his side had chances to win before Goncalo Ramos' stoppage-time winner.

title: ‎He failed 35 govt exams before cracking UPSC: Read the inspiring story of Vijay Vardhan
content: Vijay Vardhan's extraordinary journey saw him fail 35 government exams before achieving his dream. After years of relentless effort and heartbreak, he finally cracked the UPSC, first becoming an IPS officer in 2018 and then an IAS officer in 2021 with an impressive rank. His story is a testament to unparalleled perseverance, inspiring countless aspirants facing similar challenges in India's competitive exam landscape.

title: 'Police regime': Stalin slams Tamil Nadu CM Vijay over ex DMK MLA's arrest
content: Former Chief Minister MK Stalin has slammed his successor, accusing the government of running an "action cinema-style police regime." Stalin claims the administration prioritizes arresting opposition MLAs over addressing rising crime rates, including sexual assault cases. He alleges the Chief Minister is using arrests to silence critics and maintain power through questionable means, rather than focusing on public welfare.

title: Why Delhi's EV policy poses big risk if adopted by other states
content: Delhi's new EV Policy 2026 aims to accelerate clean mobility by phasing out petrol and CNG two-wheelers by April 2028 and mandating electric three-wheelers from 2027. While offering substantial incentives, a Morgan Stanley report warns that wider adoption by other states could pose a significant challenge for automakers, especially in the two-wheeler segment. The policy includes Rs 70 billion in direct incentives and aims to install over 30,000 charging points.

title: After call with best friend, 26-yr-old woman found dead in hotel; boyfriend detained
content: A 26-year-old club dancer, Renuka, was discovered deceased by hanging in a Hyderabad hotel room on Thursday night. She had checked in with her boyfriend, Farukh, with whom she had a six-month relationship. Renuka, a mother of one, was separated from her husband. Farukh, who is married with children, has been detained for questioning as police investigate the incident.

title: 'It's karma': Tuchel backs England to script Azteca history 40 years after 'Hand of God'
content: England manager Thomas Tuchel sees a chance for destiny to favour his side at the Azteca Stadium, calling it 'karma' as they face Mexico in a crucial World Cup match.  Despite the altitude challenge, Tuchel is confident England can overcome the conditions through tactical play and mental strength.  He also highlighted the potential importance of hydration breaks, a tactic that proved useful in their previous win.

title: MP HC allows woman to end pregnancy without husband’s nod
content: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has permitted a woman to terminate her 13-week pregnancy, affirming her sole right to decide. The court ruled her consent sufficient, negating the need for her estranged husband's permission or judicial approval. Citing Supreme Court precedents, the bench emphasized a woman's autonomy over her body and reproductive choices, ensuring access to safe and legal abortions without coercion.

title: India's services sector growth falls to 17-month low as hiring stalls, demand weakens
content: India's services sector experienced a significant slowdown in June, reaching a 17-month low due to weakening domestic demand and subdued hiring. While export orders provided a bright spot, overall business activity moderated. This trend also impacted the broader private sector, with the composite PMI indicating slower growth. Despite the cooling, the sector remains in expansionary territory.

title: Why equaliser was ruled out: FIFA explains VAR drama in Portugal vs Croatia match
content: FIFA confirmed that Croatia's dramatic 103rd-minute equaliser against Portugal was correctly ruled out after Connected Ball Technology detected a slight touch by Igor Matanovic before the ball reached offside player Mario Pasalic. The decision, backed by the match ball's IMU sensor data, denied Croatia a late equaliser as Portugal advanced with a 2-1 victory.

title: Yamal shatters Mbappe's record as Spain storm into FIFA World Cup Round of 16
content: Spain's Lamine Yamal has etched his name in history, becoming the youngest European player to achieve 10 major tournament victories at just 18 years old. He surpassed Kylian Mbappe's record by a significant margin during Spain's dominant 3-0 win over Austria. Despite an injury setback, Yamal showcased his electrifying form, earning Player of the Match. Spain's flawless defensive record continues as they advance to face Portugal.

title: End of an era! Riyad Mahrez retires after Algeria's FIFA World Cup exit
content: Algeria's captain, Riyad Mahrez, has retired from international football after their World Cup exit. The 35-year-old's illustrious career, marked by 119 caps and the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations triumph, concluded with a 2-0 loss to Switzerland. Mahrez leaves behind a legacy of 40 goals and 45 assists, solidifying his status as an African football great. His departure signals the end of a significant era for Algerian football.

title: US warned Iran of alleged Israeli plot to kill negotiators; Araghchi, Ghalibaf among targets
content: US officials privately alerted Iran that Israel might target top negotiators, including Foreign Minister Araghchi and Speaker Ghalibaf, during peace talks. Washington feared such assassinations would derail diplomacy and reignite conflict, urging Israel against targeting Iran's political leadership. These warnings highlight growing US-Israel differences on Iran policy, with the US favoring negotiation while Israel advocates a tougher stance.

title: NHS UK to pay you to take a walk: Its new health policy explained
content: NHS England is launching a "marathon a month" walking challenge, rewarding adults with discounts for a daily 30-minute walk. This initiative, part of a 10-year health plan, aims to combat physical inactivity, linked to one in six deaths. While experts welcome the move, they stress it must be paired with broader prevention policies. Funding for rewards will eventually rely on corporate and philanthropic support.

title: GSEB HSC science supplementary result 2026 declared; pass percentage stands at 27.36%
content: The Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board (GSHSEB) has declared the HSC Science Supplementary Result 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 27.36%. Out of 16,120 students who appeared for the examination, 4,411 qualified. Girls outperformed boys in both A and B Groups, while regular candidates recorded better results than repeaters.

title: Designed for winter, overwhelmed by heat: Europe's housing challenge explained
content: Europe is facing intense heatwaves that have revealed the shortcomings of its traditional home designs, which are built to retain heat. Predominantly found in northern and central areas, these homes use materials such as stone and brick combined with thick insulation and multi-pane windows, resulting in extreme discomfort as temperatures soar.

title: 'When will Sooryavanshi be unleashed?': ABD disagrees with India coach's approach
content: Cricket circles are buzzing over teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's delayed India debut. While assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate advocates for a gradual integration, former Proteas captain AB de Villiers argues the Ireland series was the perfect platform. Legends like Shastri and Vaughan also voiced similar sentiments, suggesting Sooryavanshi should have been given a chance to gain international experience against a less formidable opponent.

title: 'Don't tell my wife': How Venezuelan man trapped under rubble for 8 days was rescued
content: A security guard, Hernan Alberto Gil Flores, miraculously survived eight days trapped under rubble after twin earthquakes in Venezuela. Rescuers, including international teams, painstakingly tunneled for over 100 hours, maintaining contact and providing sustenance. His survival, a beacon of hope amidst devastation, saw him emerge to a tearful reunion, offering immense relief to his family and a nation reeling from tragedy.

title: CA foundation May result 2026 toppers list: Sakshi Jain secures AIR 1 with 92.75%
content: ICAI has announced the CA Foundation May 2026 toppers along with the result declaration. Sakshi Jain from Nashik secured All India Rank 1 with 371 marks (92.75%). Ayanabbas Ajani and Radha Unmesh Mulay secured Rank 2 and 3 respectively. The merit list shows narrow score gaps among top performers. The examination was held across 609 centres in India and abroad, with 18,124 qualifiers out of 90,217 candidates, reflecting a 20.09% pass percentage overall declared ICAI.

title: Sharp rebound! India’s crude inventory hits almost 1-year high
content: According to estimates from Kpler, a global real-time data and analytics provider, India's crude oil inventory stood at 104 million barrels at the end of June, up significantly from 90.5 million barrels at the end of April. With the closure of the Strait of Hormuz during the conflict threatening nearly 40% of India's crude oil imports, refiners moved quickly to source alternative supplies from global markets.

title: SC refuses to stay bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi in husband murder case
content: The Supreme Court has declined to halt bail for Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of orchestrating her husband's murder. Sonam is currently in Shillong as per bail terms. The Meghalaya government's plea to cancel her bail, citing a minor legal citation error as insufficient grounds, was rejected by the apex court. The trial court had previously granted bail due to procedural lapses in the arrest notification.

title: Air India told to pay Rs 60,000 to elderly couple over pre-booked seat change
content: An elderly couple received Rs 60,000 compensation from Air India for a service deficiency. The airline wrongly changed their pre-booked seats, causing significant physical pain to the husband, who had hip surgery, and mental distress to his wife. This led to the husband being bedridden and missing a family function. The State Consumer Commission found the initial Rs 40,000 award insufficient for their suffering and litigation expenses.

title: Lesson from Operation Sindoor? Pakistan is raising a China-style rocket force
content: Following Operation Sindoor, Pakistan established the Army Rocket Force Command to bolster conventional strike capabilities, aiming to counter India's below-threshold responses. India's successful defense, utilizing S-400 systems, blunted Pakistani attacks during the conflict. This move highlights a shift towards non-contact warfare, with both nations re-evaluating their military strategies and procurement plans.

title: Is July 3 a federal holiday? What is open and closed on Friday ahead of Fourth of July
content: As the United States gears up for its 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, 2026, the preceding Friday, July 3, will be observed as a federal holiday. While most retail stores and postal services will operate normally, banks will be open with potential adjusted hours. The stock market, however, will be closed. State DMVs and city offices will see varied closures, so checking local schedules is advised.

title: Watch: Croatia fans throw bottles after VAR denies last-minute equaliser
content: Croatia's World Cup campaign ended in heartbreak after a 103rd-minute equaliser by Josko Gvardiol was ruled out by VAR for offside against Portugal. The decision sparked protests from Croatian fans and players. After Ivan Perisic's opener and Cristiano Ronaldo's penalty equaliser, Goncalo Ramos scored the winner, sending Portugal into the last 16.

title: Why endurance sport is no longer a niche pursuit in India
content: India's endurance movement is steadily growing beyond a niche community of runners and cyclists. Record participation at road races, the rise of run clubs and a younger generation embracing marathons, triathlons and ultra-runs reflect a changing fitness culture. IRONMAN triathlete Renee Noronha and ultra-runner Sufiya Sufi are among the athletes witnessing that change, while speaking about the preparation, patience, and consistency that endurance sports demand.
